stem: A healthcare study measures average patient recovery rate across 3 hospitals, each containing multiple departments. hospital A has 15 observations with mean 47.5 % (within-hospital SD = 6.1). hospital B has 8 observations with mean 46.1 % (within-hospital SD = 7.5). hospital C has 5 observations with mean 29.4 % (within-hospital SD = 9.5). The between-hospital standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 6.2 and 15.6 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true average patient recovery rate for hospital C, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-hospital variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for hospital C: 31.5 % (shrunk from raw 29.4). Range [30.4, 34.0] as between-hospital SD varies from 6.2 to 15.6. Pooled mean: 43.9.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 43.9 % (weighted average of 3 hospital means).
Shrinkage factor for hospital C: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/5). At σ_between=10.4: B = 0.857.
Interval [30.4, 34.0] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (29.4) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (43.9) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
